26-28.note: Karlgren's head graph 0554a is absent from current Mand2MC and BS-GSR imports.

03-56.source: Wiktionary zh-see 爽; OC inferred from the 爽 / GSR 733 series
03-56.note: Local Wiktionary data treats 𡙁 as a variant of 爽, so the missing-series head can inherit the 爽-series root.

04-39.source: Wiktionary alternative form of 乃; OC inferred from 乃 *nˤəʔ
04-39.note: Local Wiktionary data treats 迺 as an alternative form of 乃, so the missing-series head can inherit the 乃-family root.

07-15.source: Schuessler / Baxter-Sagart style OC *tje for 卮, reflected in local research notes
07-15.note: External reconstruction evidence for 卮 converges on OC *tje, so the missing-series head can use the normalized root te.

16-18.source: External OC reconstructions align 肇 with the 兆-family root *lrawʔ
16-18.note: Wiktionary confirms 肇 uses phonetic 肁, and external reconstruction evidence aligns it with the nearby 兆-series root law.

16-22.source: Schuessler 16-22 / 16-33 note plus the resolved 1149 family under 16-33
16-22.note: Schuessler flags the ;j' element in 16-22 as semantic, not phonetic, and the broader resolved 1149 family already supports the 小-series root sew.

16-38.source: Targeted GSR 1156a evidence plus local Wiktionary pronunciation data for 驫
16-38.note: Wiktionary confirms 驫 is the triple-horse graph and targeted GSR evidence points to OC pjiw, so the missing-series head can use the normalized root piw.

19-03.source: Local Wiktionary zh-see 咼 plus the hand-authored 19-04 咼 root
19-03.note: Wiktionary marks 𩰬 as a variant of 咼, and the neighboring 19-04 entry already fixes the 咼-family root as kuay.
